query: I just got handed 50,000 rows of customer churn data. I have no specific hypothesis yet — I just want to look around and see what patterns are in there. Help me get started.
expected_behavior: Does NOT engage the pre-registration workflow (does not produce a hypothesis lock, stopping rule, or power calc)
Explains that pure exploratory data analysis does not require pre-registration because no confirmatory or 'significant' claim is being made; the user is generating hypotheses, not testing them

Warns that if a specific hypothesis is found in this data and the user later wants to claim it as a confirmed finding, that hypothesis MUST be tested on a fresh, held-out, or new dataset to avoid HARKing — and at that point pre-registration applies
